sanskrit: उत्सीदेयुरिमे लोका न कुर्यां कर्म चेदहम् । सङ्करस्य च कर्ता स्यामुपहन्यामिमाः प्रजाः ॥
hindiTranslation: हे पार्थ  अगर मैं किसी समय सावधान होकर कर्तव्यकर्म न करूँ तो बड़ी हानि हो जाय क्योंकि मनुष्य सब प्रकारसे मेरे ही मार्गका अनुसरण करते हैं ।  यदि मैं कर्म न करूँ, तो ये सब मनुष्य नष्टभ्रष्ट हो जायँ और मैं वर्णसंकरताको करनेवाला तथा इस समस्त प्रजाको नष्ट करनेवाला बनूँ ॥
englishTranslation: If I did not act, these worlds would fall into ruin. I would create disorder and destroy these people.
essence: Even the highest must act to prevent disorder.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Krishna says that even he must keep acting, because if he stopped, society would fall into disorder and people would be harmed.
simpleInsight: Your actions matter beyond you. Neglecting what you must do can unsettle many lives.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna is frozen. Krishna explains that duty is not optional even for the highest being: if he stopped acting, the social order would break and people would suffer. This verse strengthens the case for steady action.
modernRelevance: You skip the hard conversation, hoping the problem will wait. It doesn't. Silence spreads confusion, and everyone around you ends up paying for your avoidance.
translationLiteral: उत्सीदेयुः (would fall into ruin) / इमे (these) / लोकाः (worlds) / न (not) / कुर्याम् (I would do) / कर्म (action) / चेत् (if) / अहम् (I) / सङ्करस्य (of disorder / mixed confusion) / च (and) / कर्ता (maker) / स्याम् (I would become) / उपहन्याम् (I would destroy) / इमाः (these) / प्रजाः (people)
insight: It shifts duty from personal preference to responsibility for the wider order.
